title: Roasted Korean Peppers Bowl
description: A flavorful and nutritious meal-prep dish featuring roasted mixed peppers, perfect for a weeknight dinner
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, brown sugar, rice vinegar, and crushed red pepper flakes.
In a large bowl, toss the mixed peppers with coconut oil, garlic, ginger, and soy sauce mixture until they are evenly coated.
Spread the peppers in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast for 25 minutes, or until tender and slightly charred.
While the peppers are roasting, cook the green onions in a small pan with a little water until softened.
Fluff the cooked peppers with a spatula and top with cooked green onions, toasted sesame seeds, and any additional seasonings you like.
Serve warm or at room temperature, garnished with additional green onions and sesame seeds if desired.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or in the freezer for up to 2 months.
Reheat in the oven or microwave until warm and steaming hot.
To make this recipe more substantial, add cooked brown rice, quinoa, or noodles to the bowl.
For a spicy kick, add more crushed red pepper flakes or sliced jalape1os.
For a different flavor, swap out the soy sauce for tamari or nama shoyu.
For a vegan version, omit the sesame seeds or replace with sesame oil.
For a gluten-free version, use gluten-free soy sauce or tamari.
To make ahead, roast the peppers and store them in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
To make this recipe more colorful, add diced carrots, zucchini, or bell peppers to the bowl.
To make this recipe more filling, add cooked chickpeas, black beans, or roasted sweet potatoes to the bowl.
To make this recipe more flavorful, add grated ginger, minced garlic, or sliced green onions to the bowl.
